title: The One With Joeys Interview

jokes.text: That's me! I'm blank!
jokes.setup: Joey discovers he's a crossword clue
jokes.punchline: Joey's childlike excitement at being 'blank' - something most people wouldn't celebrate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his genuine pride in something trivial shows his sweet naivety

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm a crossword clue? What am I?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're 'blank' - four letters.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm a crossword clue! That's so cool! I'm gonna be in the New York Times!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, the clue is 'blank.' You're the answer to 'blank.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So I'm blank?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey. You're blank.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's awesome!

jokes.text: How cool! We know three down. I'm touching three down.
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to share Joey's excitement
jokes.punchline: Innocent comment becomes unintentionally suggestive
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo that's more accidental than clever

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, that's so exciting! Tell me everything about it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, it all started when I went in for the audition...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: And then what? Don't leave me hanging, I want to hear how it went all the way.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: Yeah, you are, baby. Three down knows I'm married. What's three down doing?
jokes.setup: Ross hears Rachel's innocent comment
jokes.punchline: Ross gets jealous of a crossword puzzle clue
jokes.explanation: Great character-driven absurdity - Ross's possessiveness extends to crossword puzzles

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I spent the whole evening working on this crossword puzzle.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: All evening? What crossword puzzle is so interesting?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's really challenging. There's this one clue I just can't figure out.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Let me see it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's a five-letter word for 'incredibly attractive.'

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Who came up with this puzzle?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know, but I've been staring at it for an hour.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You've been staring at it for an hour? Rachel, we have plans!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, it's just a crossword puzzle.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Just a crossword puzzle? You said you spent the whole evening with it, you couldn't figure it out, and now you're staring at it? I'm standing right here!

jokes.text: If only there was something in your head to control what you say.
jokes.setup: Joey worries about saying something stupid in interviews
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests Joey could use his brain
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect timing and economy

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I worry I'm going to say something stupid in interviews.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, have you thought about using your brain?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My brain?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's that thing in your head that tells you what to say.

jokes.text: Is that 'gal pal' spelled L-O-S-E-R?
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests Joey mention 'gal pal Rachel Green'
jokes.punchline: Ross spells out 'loser' as if it's an alternate spelling
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sounds like helpful spelling but delivers an insult

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's L-O-S-E-R.

jokes.text: Just then, or all the time? Because we have jobs, you know?
jokes.setup: Joey asks friends to stop him if he says something stupid
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies Joey always says stupid things
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'Joey says stupid things' running theme

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, if I ever say something stupid, just stop me, okay?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, we'd never get to eat!

jokes.text: No. We're just four people with neck problems, who talk like this.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if they're being obvious (presumably craning necks to watch)
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically explains their conspicuous behavior
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy made verbal - acknowledges the absurdity of their spying position

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, we're being very subtle. We're just gonna stand here and crane our necks like we're watching a tennis match.

jokes.text: And I'll have all the muffins.
jokes.setup: Joey orders coffee after claiming he does interviews for fans, not free food
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself by ordering excessive food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey hypocrisy - noble words immediately followed by gluttony

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll have a coffee, black.

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: And for food?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, I'm not hungry. I just do interviews for fans, not for the free food.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? I'll take a turkey sandwich, a tuna melt, some fries, a piece of pie, and a bowl of soup.

jokes.text: We're not talking. Oh, finally.
jokes.setup: Monica declares they're not talking
jokes.punchline: Chandler's relieved 'finally' reveals he wanted the silent treatment
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation perfectly - we expect him to be upset, but he's relieved

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We're not talking.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Finally!

jokes.text: All you want is a dingle / What you envy's a shwang / A thing through which you can tinkle / Or play with, or simply let hang
jokes.setup: Phoebe performing what appears to be a song
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemisms for male anatomy in song form
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Phoebe absurdity - innocent delivery of inappropriate content

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My sticky shoe is on your foot, and my sticky shoe is on the ground. Cowboy gee, what'll I do? My sticky shoe is on your foot.

jokes.text: Boy, you said it, Mike.
jokes.setup: Joey appears to be in a commercial about hard-to-open milk cartons
jokes.punchline: Joey's overenthusiastic endorsement of the problem
jokes.explanation: Standard infomercial parody - Joey's acting is the joke

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what I hate? When you're pouring milk and it just keeps coming and coming and coming... and you can't stop it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, now there's a solution: New Miracle Carton!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's impossible to open!

jokes.text: I'm gonna get on this spaceship and I'm gonna go to Blargon 7 in search of alternative fuels.
jokes.setup: Joey appears to be in a sci-fi soap opera scene
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous planet name 'Blargon 7' delivered with soap opera seriousness
jokes.explanation: Silly sci-fi name paired with environmental message creates soap opera absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm afraid I have some bad news. The readings on the planetary sensors indicate that we have less than 48 hours before Blargon 7 destroys itself.

jokes.text: You know, that's bad for the paper tray.
jokes.setup: Joey watches people have sex on a copier
jokes.punchline: His only concern is the office equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - completely wrong priority in a sexual situation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! You're gonna break the copier!

jokes.text: I was just being polite.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers to get coffee, others place elaborate orders
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission he wasn't actually offering
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of social awkwardness we all recognize

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll get coffee. What do you guys want?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, can you get me a cappuccino with an extra shot?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'll have a venti latte, no-fat milk, one Sweet'N Low.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Just a regular coffee, but can you make sure it's fresh?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Large coffee with lots of cream and sugar.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'll have a herbal tea, chamomile if they have it.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I wasn't actually offering.

jokes.text: Who names their boat Coast Guard, anyway?
jokes.setup: Joey yells at what he thinks is a boat named 'Coast Guard'
jokes.punchline: He doesn't realize it's the actual Coast Guard
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness - missing obvious context clues

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! Coast Guard! Yeah, you! What are you doing?!

dialogue.speaker: Coast Guard Officer
dialogue.line: Sir, this is the actual Coast Guard.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh... oh no.

jokes.text: What are they doing out here? The coast is way over there!
jokes.setup: Joey learns it's the Coast Guard
jokes.punchline: Joey questions their geographic logic
jokes.explanation: Doubles down on the misunderstanding with Joey's literal thinking

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. The Coast Guard? What do they guard? The coast, right?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So what happens if there's a problem in like, Montana? They just like, ignore it?

jokes.text: Isn't this a woman's hat?
jokes.setup: Ross appears to be wearing a hat
jokes.punchline: Ross questions the hat's gender appropriateness
jokes.explanation: Basic gender-based humor without much sophistication

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? What's wrong with the hat?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, that's a woman's hat.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No it's not!

jokes.text: Dude, stop talking crazy and make us some tea.
jokes.setup: Ross questions the hat
jokes.punchline: Chandler dismisses his concern and gives him domestic tasks
jokes.explanation: Mild role reversal joke about masculine/feminine roles

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What's with the hat?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I like the hat. Now, are you gonna help me move this couch or not?

jokes.text: Free porn! We have free porn here!
jokes.setup: They're trying to turn off porn but discover they still have it
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning their resolution with childlike excitement
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how easily they abandon their good intentions

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. We're not deleting it?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Are you kidding? Did you see what we have?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Double feature!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hit play!

jokes.text: A mento? You know, a mento. A role model.
jokes.setup: Joey says he's a 'mento' for kids
jokes.punchline: Joey mispronounces 'mentor' as 'mento'
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey malapropism that he doubles down on confidently

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm a mento for kids.

jokes.text: Like the candy? As a matter of fact, I do.
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks if he means 'like the candy'
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks she's asking if he likes Mentos candy
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the mento/mentor confusion with confident misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: Like the candy?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I like Mentos.

jokes.text: That's the first time I've ever missed a meal. My pants are a little loose.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes they forgot about lunch during their throwing game
jokes.punchline: Joey's pants are already loose from missing one meal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character beat - his relationship with food is so intense he's physically affected immediately

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, we've been playing for how long? I forgot about lunch!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My pants are already getting loose. One more hour and I'll have nothing to hold them up.

jokes.text: I haven't been able to stand up since. But I don't think it's serious.
jokes.setup: Chandler describes having blinding pain and passing out
jokes.punchline: Despite being unable to stand, he doesn't think it's serious
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - obvious contradiction between symptoms and assessment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't stand up, I can't see anything, I think I'm gonna pass out.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, that's serious!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm sure it's nothing.

jokes.text: Why'd I have to start working out again? Damn you, fifteens!
jokes.setup: Chandler is injured from working out
jokes.punchline: Blames fifteen-pound weights as if they're heavy
jokes.explanation: Reveals Chandler's lack of fitness by treating light weights as formidable opponents

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't believe I got injured from fifteen-pound weights. Fifteen pounds! That's like... really heavy.

jokes.text: Raspberries, more ladyfingers, beef sautéed with peas and onions.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes layers of her trifle
jokes.punchline: Meat appears unexpectedly in what should be a dessert
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for the trifle disaster - casual mention of beef in dessert

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It starts with a layer of ladyfingers, then custard, then jam, then beef sautéed with peas and onions, then a little more custard, then bananas, and then I just put some whipped cream on top.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You put beef in a trifle?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it would give it a nice flavor!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: When I'm eating a dessert, I don't like to come across some beef!

jokes.text: It tastes like feet.
jokes.setup: Everyone tries Rachel's trifle
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt, disgusted assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective gross-out reaction that's become iconic

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What is this? It tastes like feet!

jokes.text: I like it.
jokes.setup: Ross says the trifle tastes like feet
jokes.punchline: Joey genuinely likes the disgusting dish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his indiscriminate eating habits make him enjoy the abomination

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, what are you guys talking about? This is delicious!

jokes.text: What's not to like? Custard, good. Jam, good. Meat, good!
jokes.setup: Others are horrified by the trifle
jokes.punchline: Joey's simple logic about individual ingredients
jokes.explanation: Became an iconic Friends quote - Joey's childlike food logic delivered with perfect confidence

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey. What's all the fuss? It's got all the main food groups: fruit, custard, cake, jam, and beef.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Beef?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, beef. What? It's a trifle.

jokes.text: Cold cuts, ice cream, limes. Hey, what was in that brown jar?
jokes.setup: Joey explains he ate everything when the fridge broke
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions eating something unidentifiable and brown
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that escalates Joey's indiscriminate eating

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, when the fridge broke, I ate everything. There was this thing... brown, unidentifiable... I don't even know what it was, but it tasted like... regret.

jokes.text: This piece doesn't have floor on it.
jokes.setup: Joey continues eating the trifle
jokes.punchline: Implies other pieces have floor in them, as if that's the only problem
jokes.explanation: Reveals the trifle has literally fallen on the floor, but Joey's only concerned with clean pieces

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? There's only a little bit of floor in here.

jokes.text: I'll take those to go. For the kids.
jokes.setup: Joey orders muffins after claiming he doesn't care about free food
jokes.punchline: Transparent lie about taking them for kids
jokes.explanation: Nice callback combining Joey's gluttony with his 'mento' lie

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't care about free food.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll take a dozen muffins for my kids.

jokes.text: This might be Joey's baby, who knows?
jokes.setup: Rachel tries to be memorable in the interview
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately suggests uncertain paternity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Rachel's desperation leads to inappropriate oversharing

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So I'm thinking, what makes someone memorable in an interview? You have to stand out, right?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So I leaned across the desk and I said, 'You know what? I think your baby might not be yours.'

jokes.text: Who just lost the respect of her unborn child.
jokes.setup: Rachel makes inappropriate paternity joke
jokes.punchline: Ross suggests even the fetus is disappointed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - anthropomorphizing the fetus for comedic judgment

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm pregnant and even the fetus is disappointed in me.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, at least you're consistent.

jokes.text: I'd like to say that Joey truly has enriched the days of our lives.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to say something nice about Joey
jokes.punchline: References Joey's soap opera 'Days of Our Lives'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay referencing the show within the show

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, Joey's a great guy. He's caring, he's thoughtful...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: On 'Days of Our Lives,' his character saved an entire hospital!

jokes.text: It's P as in Phoebe. H as in Heebie. O as in Obie. E as in Ebie. B as in Beebee. And E as in, ''ello there, mate!'
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks how to spell Phoebe's name
jokes.punchline: Phoebe uses nonsensical words that don't help with spelling
jokes.explanation: Peak Phoebe absurdity - completely defeats the purpose of phonetic spelling

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: How do you spell your name?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: P-H-O-E-B-E. It's like... P as in Phoebe, H as in... Huge, O as in... Oh, E as in... Eeew, B as in... Bee, E as in... Eeew again.

jokes.text: This isn't just about jewelry. It's about you and me and the fact that we're best buds.
jokes.setup: Chandler gives Joey a bracelet
jokes.punchline: Overly earnest declaration of friendship over a piece of jewelry
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe from Chandler's uncharacteristic emotional openness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I got you a bracelet. It's a symbol of our friendship.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler, this is the nicest thing anyone's ever done for me.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's just a bracelet, Joe.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, it's more than that. It's a bond. A physical representation of the bond between two men who love each other.

jokes.text: Check it out! We're bracelet buddies! That's what they'll call us!
jokes.setup: Chandler continues being overly enthusiastic about the bracelets
jokes.punchline: Creates a cringeworthy nickname for their friendship
jokes.explanation: Escalates the cringe with an embarrassing friendship nickname

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: These bracelets are amazing! We're basically best friends now. From now on, we're the Bracelet Brothers!

jokes.text: Well, don't waste it. I mean, it's still food. Jeez!
jokes.setup: Someone spills food in Joey's messy apartment
jokes.punchline: Joey's horrified at wasting food, not the mess
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey priorities - mess doesn't matter but wasting food is unforgivable

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey! Watch the food!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't care about the mess, but that's a perfectly good sandwich!

jokes.text: Get your foot off my contestant! Judge! Judge rules: No violation.
jokes.setup: Ross and Monica appear to be racing pets
jokes.punchline: Overly competitive sibling behavior with made-up rules
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ross and Monica's competitive nature

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: My turtle is faster than your cat!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's because you're letting him cheat! He's not even staying in his lane!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: There is no lane, Monica. We never established lanes.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, so NOW you make up the rules as you go along? Just like when we were kids!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was six years old!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And apparently you haven't matured since!

jokes.text: You can't say that! You don't know!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions seeing something on Discovery Channel about jellyfish
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive reaction suggests embarrassing personal experience
jokes.explanation: Sets up the jellyfish sting story with Monica's embarrassed denial

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I was curious! It was one time, and the doctor said it was a very common injury!

jokes.text: That's right, I stepped up! She's my friend and she needed help! If I had to, I'd pee on any one of you!
jokes.setup: Joey helped Monica with a jellyfish sting using urine
jokes.punchline: Joey's proud declaration of loyalty through urination
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends moment - Joey's noble friendship gesture becomes hilariously inappropriate offer

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm there for you, bro.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And that, my friends, is the most romantic thing anyone's ever said.

jokes.text: Wow, I don't even know any huge gay people.
jokes.setup: Interviewer mentions Joey's huge gay fan base
jokes.punchline: Joey misunderstands 'huge' as referring to physical size
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey misunderstanding - literal interpretation of figurative language

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: You have a huge gay fan base.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! I don't know what you're talking about. I'm not gay!

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: No, no, I mean your gay fans—they love you.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, that's cool. But they're not huge. I mean, some of them are big guys, but...

jokes.text: It hurts me. It physically hurts me.
jokes.setup: Joey's misunderstanding about his gay fan base
jokes.punchline: Chandler's pained reaction to Joey's obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Great reaction beat - Chandler's physical pain from secondhand embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Chandler, I've been thinking about my fan base. A lot of gay guys seem to really like me.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's... that's great, Joe.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I think it's because I'm so in touch with my feminine side, you know? I'm sensitive, I'm caring...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: *winces* Oh my God.

jokes.text: What? Are you referring to my man's bag?
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks if Joey is gay (off the record)
jokes.punchline: Joey defensively mentions his 'man's bag' as if that's relevant
jokes.explanation: Joey's defensive response misses the point entirely

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: Off the record, are you gay?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No! I'm not gay. I have a man's bag.

jokes.text: When we said 'face to face,' we didn't think it all the way through!
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler are stuck together climbing down
jokes.punchline: They realize their plan put them in an awkward position
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with predictable awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, so if you're on my shoulders and I'm holding onto the fire escape...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How exactly are you going to reach that window?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I... I don't know. I didn't think this through.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You didn't think it through? Chandler, your butt is in my face!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, this isn't my favorite position either, Ross!

jokes.text: Nice. Manly and also kind of a slut.
jokes.setup: Someone shows off their appearance
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's backhanded compliment combines positive and negative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe observation - blunt honesty delivered as a compliment

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wow, you look great! You know, for someone who's been through so much.

jokes.text: Now I've upset you? What did I say? Not what you said, it's just the way you said it. Oh, my God! I'm a woman!
jokes.setup: Chandler is accused of becoming feminine
jokes.punchline: His emotional reaction proves the point
jokes.explanation: Good escalating realization - Chandler's feminine response confirms the accusation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I am NOT feminine!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: *voice cracks* Could I BE any more masculine?!

jokes.text: Hey. How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrates his pickup technique
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey catchphrase moment - not original but perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How you doin'?

jokes.text: Don't!
jokes.setup: Joey starts to use his pickup line on Rachel's sister
jokes.punchline: Ross's panicked intervention
jokes.explanation: Good panic reaction but simple execution

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So, do you have a map? I just keep losing myself in your eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Joey, can I talk to you for a second?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I'm just being friendly.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, friendly. That's... that's great. But maybe we should all just, you know, hang out as a group?

jokes.text: What? Oh, dear God!
jokes.setup: Joey realizes his pickup line worked on Rachel's sister
jokes.punchline: His horror at his own success
jokes.explanation: Joey's shocked reaction to his line actually working in this context

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, it worked? Oh no, oh no, oh no. What do I do now? I don't even remember what I said!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're Joey Tribbiani. You always know what to do.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Not with someone's sister! What if she tells Rachel? What if Rachel tells Monica? What if Monica tells Chandler?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm standing right here.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know! That's how bad this is!

jokes.text: Oh, I don't watch soap operas. Excuse me? I have a life, you know?
jokes.setup: Interviewer asks Joey's favorite soap opera
jokes.punchline: Joey insults soap operas despite being on one
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Joey insults his own profession

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: What's your favorite soap opera?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Soap operas? Please. They're so unrealistic. Nobody talks like that, the plots make no sense, and the acting is terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: But you're on 'Days of Our Lives.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well... that one's different.

jokes.text: You slept with her, didn't you? A little bit.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks if Joey slept with the interviewer
jokes.punchline: Joey's nonsensical response 'a little bit'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - quantifying something that can't be quantified

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Did you sleep with the interviewer?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A little bit.

jokes.text: That's just a little something for my huge gay fan base.
jokes.setup: Joey references his earlier misunderstanding about gay fans
jokes.punchline: Shows he still doesn't understand what 'huge' meant
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Joey still doesn't get it

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, so when you said I had huge gay fans, you didn't mean like, physically huge?

jokes.text: Did you just wink at me? You're the one who loves the picture.
jokes.setup: Joey winks after mentioning his gay fan base
jokes.punchline: Joey's defensive response to Chandler's question
jokes.explanation: Weak ending - doesn't fully land the joke about Joey's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you have a gay fan base?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, so what? There's nothing wrong with that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, it's great. I'm just wondering... does David Schwimmer know?

tmdb_overview: Joey prepares for an interview with Soap Opera Digest, although he's afraid of saying something stupid and getting himself into trouble, so he enlists his friends to make sure things go well. During the interview, he reminisces about his past acting experiences, his friends and his romantic life.
insight: Joey's interview episode lands 54 jokes in 22 minutes but never breaks 77—character work alone can't carry it.
